title: Switch a motor with a transistor
description: Use an NPN transistor to let a small base current control a much larger current through a DC motor — the core idea behind every transistor switch.
author: Breadboard Sim Team

steps.instructions: Place the battery — this powers the whole circuit.
Add the DC motor between the + rail and column 10 — this is the load the transistor will switch on and off.
Place the NPN transistor. Collector picks up the motor's free end (column 10), base is the control input (column 12), emitter will return to ground.
Wire the emitter to the − rail to complete the ground return.
Add a base resistor from the + rail to the transistor's base column. This is the small control current that switches the much larger motor current.

Hit Simulate and watch the motor spin. Click the transistor in the Inspector — Ib (base current) is tiny compared to Ic (collector/motor current). That amplification is the whole point of a transistor switch.
